input_options.title: Input Options
input_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and change some frequently used options.

fasta.title: FASTA
fasta.help_text: You can provide either a single '.fasta' or '.fa' file, or one folder which will be searched for all '.fasta' and '.fa' files.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Input metadata file(s) for your fasta file.
metadata.help_text: This is the path to one metadata file or one folder containing metadata files. Metadata file extensions must be one or more of '.csv', '.tsv' or '.tab'. Caution: If given a directory it will try to parse ALL files with these file extensions so ensure only appropriate files are in the folder.

seq_id_delimiter.title: ID delimiter
seq_id_delimiter.help_text: The ID delimiter is the character that separates each element in the headers provided in your fasta files.

seq_id_field_index.title: ID field
seq_id_field_index.description: Choose the 0-based index position of the ID in your input headers (default: 0)
seq_id_field_index.help_text: If the header is split using the delimiter from 'ID delimiter' above, choose the 0-based position of the sequence ID that can be used to match the Metadata ID.

metadata_delimiter.title: Metadata Delimiter
metadata_delimiter.description: Metadata file delimiter (default: `,`). Will auto-detect and parse .tsv/.tab files
metadata_delimiter.help_text: Explicitly choose what delimiter to use when parsing the metadata files.

pipeline_options.title: Pipeline Options
pipeline_options.description: Configure optional settings within the raccoon pipeline.

alignment_only.title: Stop workflow after alignment steps
alignment_only.description: Stop workflow after alignment step so phylogenetics is not performed. (default: Run entire pipeline).
alignment_only.help_text: If selected, the seq-qc, mafft alignment, and aln-qc steps will run and the workflow will end. A mask file will be produced as per the flags in 'Alignment QC Options' but will not be applied to your alignment.

run_mask.title: Run masking step
run_mask.description: Mask the input alignment before running IQ-TREE (default: Do NOT mask).
run_mask.help_text: By default, the alignment generated from your input fasta files will NOT have problematic sites masked. If masking is not selected (default), a mask file will still be generated as per the flags in 'Alignment QC Options' but it will not be applied to the sequence prior to tree building.

tree_model.title: IQTREE substitution model
tree_model.description: Choose which substitution model IQTREE will use to reconstruct a phylogenetic tree.
tree_model.help_text: The default, TEST, will make IQTREE run the model selection step and automatically choose its preferred model. The 'MFP' option will run the model selection step with the addition of FreeRate models

outgroup.title: Outgroup
outgroup.description: Sequence header for the outgroup to be used in tree building (default: no outgroup)
outgroup.help_text: This needs to match the sequence header of the outgroup after any header manipulation by seq-qc. For example if there was no metadata for the outgroup this may look like 'IDXXXX||'. This will also be used to prune the outgroup from the resulting tree.

midpoint_root.title: Midpoint root
midpoint_root.description: Midpoint root the resulting tree for report visualisation (Default: True if no outgroup provided).
midpoint_root.help_text: This is only applied when an outgroup has NOT been provided, as otherwise Ancestral State Reconstruction is run which requires trees to be outgroup rooted.

threads.title: Maximum number of CPU threads
threads.description: Maximum number of CPU threads to use per workflow task.
threads.help_text: Several tasks in this workflow benefit from using multiple CPU threads. This option sets the number of CPU threads for all such processes. The total CPU resource used by the workflow is constrained by the executor configuration. See server threads parameter for kraken specific threads.

seq_qc.title: Sequence QC Options
seq_qc.description: Change settings for the raccoon seq-qc module.

min_length.title: Minimum sequence length to keep
min_length.description: Filter out sequences that are shorter than this value
min_length.help_text: Filter out sequences that are shorter than this value. This is the minimum length a sequence must have to be kept in the analysis. For example, if your sequences are around 30,000bp and you set this to 29,000, any sequences shorter than 29,000bp will be filtered out.

max_n_content.title: Maximum N content
max_n_content.description: Maximum N content proportion to keep (e.g. 0.2 for 20% N content).
max_n_content.help_text: Valid float between 0 and 1. Filter out sequences that have an N content higher than this value. This is the proportion of the sequence that can be made up of Ns. For example, if your sequences are around 30,000bp and you set this to 0.2, any sequences with more than 6000 Ns will be filtered out.

header_fields.title: Header fields
header_fields.description: Preconfigured template for constructing harmonised sequence headers ( e.g. {sample}|{location}|{date} ).
header_fields.help_text: Choose from the common header templates provided or build a custom one in the 'Custom header template' option below.

custom_header_fields.title: Custom header template
custom_header_fields.description: Template for constructing a custom sequence header structure not listed above ( e.g. {sampleID}|{country}|{health_zone}|{collection_date} ).
custom_header_fields.help_text: Use this to generate new headers based on the columns in your metadata files. Surround each column title with {} and place your delimiter of choice between each entry. Overrides header separator option.

metadata_id_field.title: Metadata ID field
metadata_id_field.help_text: Provide the header of the ID column in the provided metadata file.

metadata_location_field.title: Metadata Location field
metadata_location_field.help_text: Provide the header of the location column in the provided metadata file.

metadata_date_field.title: Metadata Date field
metadata_date_field.help_text: Provide the header of the date column in the provided metadata file.

header_separator.title: Header separator
header_separator.help_text: The header separator is the character that will separate each element in the newly generated FASTA header.

aln_qc.title: Alignment QC Options
aln_qc.description: Change settings for the raccoon aln-qc module.

flag_removal_threshold.title: Flag for removal threshold
flag_removal_threshold.description: The threshold of flagged SNPSs above which sequences will be flagged for removal (default: 20 SNPs).
flag_removal_threshold.help_text: If a sequence has over this number of potentially probelmatic SNPs flagged, it will be flagged as a sequence to remove from your analysis rather than masking specific sites in the alignment.

no_flag_clustered.title: Do not flag clustered SNPs
no_flag_clustered.help_text: Choose to flag clustered snps or not. Can be used to mask resulting alignment.

no_flag_n_adjacent.title: Do not flag N adjacent SNPs
no_flag_n_adjacent.description: Skip flagging SNPs adjacent to Ns (default: False).
no_flag_n_adjacent.help_text: Choose to flag any SNPs next to Ns in your alignment. Can be used to mask resulting alignment.

no_flag_gap_adjacent.title: Do not flag gap adjacent SNPs
no_flag_gap_adjacent.description: Skip flagging SNPs adjacent to gaps (default: False).
no_flag_gap_adjacent.help_text: Choose to flag any SNPs next to gaps (-) in your alignment. Can be used to mask resulting alignment.

no_flag_frame_break.title: Do not flag frame breaks
no_flag_frame_break.description: Skip flagging any frame-breaking indels (default: False).
no_flag_frame_break.help_text: Choose to flag any insertions or deletions that lead to reading frame breaks or not. Can be used to mask resulting alignment. Requires a genbank file. CURRENTLY UNSUPPORTED IN EPI2ME - IGNORED

cluster_window.title: Cluster window size
cluster_window.description: Window size for clustered SNP detection (default: 10bp).

cluster_count.title: Cluster count
cluster_count.description: The number of SNPs within the cluster window that will be flagged as clustered SNPs (default: 3).

tree_qc.title: Tree QC Options
tree_qc.description: Change settings for the raccoon tree-qc module.

run_apobec.title: Check for APOBEC3-type mutations
run_apobec.help_text: Choose to run APOBEC3 phylo checks or not. An outgroup sequence is required to run this check as ancestral state reconstruction is performed.

run_adar.title: Check for ADAR-type mutations
run_adar.help_text: Choose to run ADAR phylo checks or not. An outgroup sequence is required to run this check as ancestral state reconstruction is performed.

adar_window.title: ADAR window size
adar_window.description: The span in base pairs for ADAR cluster window (default: 300).
adar_window.help_text: Specify the maximum distance in base pairs for the ADAR cluster window.

adar_min_count.title: Minimum ADAR sites count
adar_min_count.description: Minimum ADAR-type mutations within the ADAR window to flag a branch (default: 3).

long_branch_sd.title: Long branch Standard deviation
long_branch_sd.description: Standard deviation threshold for long branch flagging (default: 3.0).

output_options.title: Output Options
output_options.description: Parameters for saving and naming workflow outputs.

out_dir.title: Output folder name
out_dir.description: Directory for output of all user-facing files.

fig_height.title: Tree figure height
fig_height.description: Adjust the output tree figure height.

miscellaneous_options.title: Miscellaneous Options
miscellaneous_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.
